engineGuide.h2: Nissan Versa Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Nissan Versa across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: The HR16DE powered most UK-imported Versa models, offering reliable naturally aspirated performance but known for timing chain and oil consumption issues at higher mileage.
compatibleVariants: 1.6 S, 1.6 SV, 1.6 SL, 1.6 Acenta, 1.6 N-Connecta, 1.6 Tekna, 1.6 Visia
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear causing rattling on cold start (60k-100k miles)
Excessive oil consumption due to piston ring wear (80k+ miles)
Variable valve timing solenoid issues affecting performance
entries.cta: Get quotes for HR16DE Nissan Versa engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol
entries.history: The MR18DE offered increased power for select N17 Versa variants, featuring dual VTC technology but sharing some failure patterns with the HR16DE.
commonFailures: VTC actuator failure causing timing noise and rough running
Timing chain tensioner wear similar to HR16DE
entries.cta: Get quotes for MR18DE Nissan Versa engine replacement

entries.title: 1.0L Turbo Petrol
entries.history: The HRA2 DIG-T represents Nissan's downsized turbo approach for the N18 Versa, delivering improved efficiency with direct injection and turbocharging.
compatibleVariants: 1.0 DIG-T Acenta, 1.0 DIG-T N-Connecta, 1.0 DIG-T Tekna
commonFailures: Turbocharger lag and actuator issues at higher mileage
Carbon buildup on direct injection components
entries.cta: Get quotes for HRA2 Nissan Versa engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Nissan Versa Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Nissan Versa's HR16DE petrol and K9K dCi diesel engines dominate UK replacement enquiries, with timing chain and turbo issues most frequently reported. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your import Versa.

problems.h4: HR16DE Timing Chain Rattling - Why Nissan Versa 1.6L Engines Fail
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Versa 1.6 S, SV, SL, Acenta, N-Connecta (2011-2022, HR16DE)
problems.rootCause: Timing chain tensioner wear allows chain slack, causing rattling noise on cold start and potential timing jump.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner and guides before chain damage occurs
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents catastrophic failure if caught early; 2-4 year extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full timing chain kit replacement with updated tensioner design
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses root cause; 3-5 year expected lifespan with maintenance

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt HR16DE with upgraded tensioner, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term value for high-mileage imports; eliminates repeat failure risk

problems.recommendation: On a Nissan Versa worth £3,000-£6,000, a rebuilt HR16DE engine from £950 often represents better value than a £800+ timing chain repair, especially given the risk of repeat failure. We recommend comparing rebuilt engine quotes before committing to major repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt HR16DE engine prices for your Nissan Versa

problems.h4: HR16DE Excessive Oil Consumption - Why Nissan Versa Engines Burn Oil
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Versa 1.6 S, SV, SL (2011-2019, HR16DE N17 chassis)
problems.rootCause: Piston ring wear allows oil to enter combustion chamber, causing blue smoke and frequent top-ups.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Higher viscosity oil, more frequent top-ups
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary symptom management only; does not fix root cause

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine removal, partial strip, new rings and gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses root cause but labour-intensive; 6-12 month expected lifespan

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt HR16DE with upgraded piston rings, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most cost-effective solution for high-mileage engines; eliminates oil consumption

problems.recommendation: Piston ring replacement often costs nearly as much as a full rebuilt engine. For high-mileage HR16DE units, we typically recommend replacement over repair - you get upgraded components, warranty coverage, and peace of mind.
problems.cta: Get quotes for HR16DE Nissan Versa engine replacement with oil consumption fix

problems.h4: K9K dCi Turbo Failure - Why Nissan Versa Diesel Engines Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Versa 1.5 dCi Acenta, N-Connecta, Tekna (2011-present, K9K)
problems.rootCause: Turbocharger oil seal wear and carbon buildup on variable vanes cause boost loss, smoke, and eventual failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: EGR clean, oil change, turbo inspection
repairOptions.longevity: May delay failure on early symptoms; not a permanent fix

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, refurbish or replace turbocharger with warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses immediate failure; 2-4 year lifespan if supporting systems maintained

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt K9K with inspected turbo, DPF compatibility, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when turbo cost approaches engine replacement; includes all supporting components

problems.recommendation: Turbo replacement costs (£600-£1,000) often approach the price of a full rebuilt K9K engine (£1,100+). For diesel Versa owners, we recommend comparing full engine quotes - you get DPF compatibility verification and comprehensive warranty coverage.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt K9K diesel engine prices for your Nissan Versa

problems.h4: MR18DE VTC Actuator Failure - Why Nissan Versa 1.8L Engines Make Noise
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Versa 1.8 S, SV (2011-2019, MR18DE N17 chassis)
problems.rootCause: VTC solenoid wear causes timing misalignment, resulting in rattling noise, rough running, and reduced performance.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty solenoid before actuator damage occurs
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents further damage if caught early; 1-3 year extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace full VTC actuator assembly with updated part
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses root cause; 3-5 year expected lifespan with maintenance

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt MR18DE with upgraded VTC components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term solution for high-mileage units; eliminates repeat VTC issues

problems.recommendation: The MR18DE is less common in UK Versa imports, so parts availability can be limited. If your 1.8L Versa has VTC noise, we recommend confirming rebuilt engine availability before committing to actuator repair.
problems.cta: Check MR18DE Nissan Versa engine availability and pricing

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Nissan Versa
finalCta.paragraph: Timing chain rattling, oil consumption, turbo failure, or VTC issues don't have to mean the end for your import Versa. Rather than facing expensive dealer repairs or uncertain independent quotes, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Nissan Versa specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, bench testing, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - with nationwide delivery and supply & fit options available. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Nissan Versa Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Nissan Versa and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Nissan Versa Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Nissan Versa is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Nissan Versa 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5L K9K dCi was the sole diesel option across N17 and N18 Versa generations (2011-2023), offering strong motorway fuel economy and torque, though DPF and turbo maintenance awareness is essential for UK urban driving patterns.
commonFailurePoints: DPF clogging on short-journey vehicles at higher mileage
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan Versa 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: Nissan Versa 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6L HR16DE naturally aspirated petrol was the most widely fitted engine across Versa UK imports (2011-2022), delivering reliable performance with good fuel economy for urban use, though timing chain tensioner wear and oil consumption are known concerns on higher-mileage examples.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain rattling on cold start
excessive oil consumption due to piston ring wear at 80k+ miles
items.cta: Compare Nissan Versa 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Nissan Versa 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.8L MR18DE petrol offered increased power for select N17 Versa variants (2011-2019), featuring dual VTC technology for improved responsiveness, though VTC actuator issues and shared timing chain concerns with the HR16DE require attention.
commonFailurePoints: VTC actuator failure causing timing noise; timing chain tensioner wear similar to HR16DE
items.cta: Compare Nissan Versa 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Nissan Versa 1.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.0L HRA2 DIG-T turbo petrol was introduced for the N18 Versa generation (2019-present) as part of downsizing efforts, delivering improved efficiency with direct injection and turbocharging, though turbo maintenance and carbon buildup awareness is essential.
commonFailurePoints: carbon buildup on direct injection components at higher mileage
items.cta: Compare Nissan Versa 1.0L petrol engine prices

engineYears.h2: Nissan Versa Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Nissan Versa production timeline, organised so UK import owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from trusted specialists.

years.description: The N17 Versa launched in the UK as a grey import, establishing the HR16DE petrol and K9K diesel as the dominant replacement engines for this model.
keyChanges: N17 chassis introduced to UK market as grey import from Middle East/Japan
HR16DE 1.6L petrol and K9K 1.5L dCi diesel established as primary engines
MR18DE 1.8L petrol fitted to select higher-spec variants
Right-hand drive configuration standard for UK imports
knownFor: HR16DE reliability but timing chain tensioner wear emerges on higher-mileage examples
K9K diesel delivers strong economy but DPF concerns on short-journey UK use
Import specification differences create fitment verification importance for replacements
years.cta: Get quotes for 2011-2014 Nissan Versa replacement engines
years.ticker: Nissan Versa HR16DE timing chain • Nissan Versa K9K turbo • Nissan Versa 1.6 engine replacement • Nissan Versa 1.5 dCi engine

years.description: Mid-production N17 Versa models saw refined engine mappings and updated emissions systems, with HR16DE remaining the most searched replacement engine for UK import owners.
keyChanges: Minor engine mapping updates for emissions compliance
HR16DE timing chain tensioner design refinements on later builds
Continued K9K diesel availability with updated DPF systems
Growing UK import volume increases replacement engine demand
mainEngines: HR16DE - 1.6L petrol (1.6 S / SV / SL / Acenta / N-Connecta)
knownFor: Later N17 HR16DE units show improved timing chain reliability vs early builds
Oil consumption becomes prominent concern on 80k+ mile HR16DE engines
K9K diesel requires DPF maintenance awareness for UK MOT compliance
1.6L petrol remains best value option for budget-conscious replacement
years.cta: Get quotes for 2015-2018 Nissan Versa replacement engines
years.ticker: Nissan Versa HR16DE oil consumption • Nissan Versa K9K DPF • Nissan Versa 1.6 SV engine • Nissan Versa 1.5 dCi replacement

years.description: The N18 Versa introduced updated styling and the new HRA2 1.0L DIG-T turbo petrol alongside continued HR16DE and K9K availability, creating three distinct engine families for replacement enquiries.
keyChanges: N18 chassis launched with updated exterior/interior design
HRA2 1.0L DIG-T turbo petrol introduced for downsized efficiency
HR16DE 1.6L continued for base/mid variants; K9K diesel retained
Enhanced emissions systems across all engine families for UK compliance
mainEngines: HRA2 DIG-T - 1.0L turbo petrol (1.0 DIG-T variants)
knownFor: HR16DE remains reliable, cost-effective replacement option for N18 base variants
HRA2 turbo petrol requires specific maintenance awareness for turbo longevity
K9K diesel continues with updated emissions compliance for UK MOT
Import specification verification remains critical for all N18 replacement engines
years.cta: Get quotes for 2019-2023 Nissan Versa replacement engines
years.ticker: Nissan Versa HRA2 turbo • Nissan Versa HR16DE N18 • Nissan Versa 1.0 DIG-T engine • Nissan Versa 1.5 dCi Tekna

faq.h2: Nissan Versa Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Nissan Versa engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt HR16DE 1.6L petrol engine typically costs £950-£1,450 supply-only, while K9K 1.5L diesel units range £1,100-£1,650. Supply & fit adds £300-£600 depending on location. All engines include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from vetted UK specialists.
keyPoints: HR16DE petrol most affordable option for Versa replacement
K9K diesel slightly higher due to turbo/DPF complexity
Supply & fit service available through approved UK fitter network
Price varies by engine condition, variant and supplier
items.cta: Compare Nissan Versa engine replacement prices for your exact variant

items.question: What engine codes fit my Nissan Versa import?
items.answer: The most common codes are HR16DE (1.6L petrol), MR18DE (1.8L petrol), K9K dCi (1.5L diesel) and HRA2 DIG-T (1.0L turbo petrol). Enter your registration above to instantly verify which code fits your specific Versa chassis and variant.
keyPoints: HR16DE fitted to most 1.6L petrol Versa imports
K9K dCi used across all 1.5L diesel variants

items.question: Will a replacement import engine affect my Nissan Versa MOT UK?
items.answer: No - engines supplied through our network include the correct emissions equipment and documentation for UK MOT compliance. Diesel K9K units retain DPF systems; petrol units meet Euro 4/5 standards as applicable to your variant.

items.question: Are used Nissan Versa import engines a good option UK?
